fitness-sparse.content:
# GHL CHAT/SMS AUTOMATION SEQUENCE
**For: Marcus Reed — Reed Performance**

---

#### STEP 1: Welcome + Qualification Start

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact sends DM keyword "READY" or initiates conversation

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds with qualification answers: → Go to Step 2
> - IF contact asks questions: → Go to Step 8 (FAQ Handler)
> - IF no response after 2 hours: → Go to Step 9 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: lead-qualified-start]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualification-stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey there! I see you're interested in getting in the best shape of your life without leaving your home office.

I'm Marcus Reed, and I help male executives lose 30+ pounds using The Performance Stack — a system that syncs to 60-hour work weeks instead of fighting them.

Before we go further, I need to ask you a few quick questions to see if you're a fit for what I do:

**Question 1:** What's your current annual revenue? (I work specifically with executives making $300K+ who understand the value of systems)

Just reply with your answer and I'll send the next question.

---

#### STEP 2: Qualification Question 2

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact provides revenue answer from Step 1

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF revenue is $300K+: → Continue to next question
> - IF revenue is under $300K: → Go to Step 10 (Polite Disqualification)
> - IF no response after 1 hour: → Go to Step 9 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: revenue-qualified] or [+Add: revenue-disqualified]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in qualification-stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. 

**Question 2:** How many fitness programs have you tried in the last 3 years that didn't stick? (I specialize in executives who've tried multiple approaches but keep hitting the same roadblocks)

---

#### STEP 3: Qualification Question 3

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact answers Question 2

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF 2+ failed attempts: → Continue to next question
> - IF 0-1 attempts: → Go to Step 11 (Not Ready Yet)
> - IF no response after 1 hour: → Go to Step 9 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: experience-qualified] or [+Add: experience-not-ready]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in qualification-stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got it.

**Question 3:** What's your biggest challenge right now — no time to train, programs that fall apart when you travel, or trainers who don't understand your schedule?

---

#### STEP 4: Qualification Question 4

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact answers Question 3

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF mentions time/travel/schedule issues: → Continue to next question
> - IF mentions other issues: → Go to Step 12 (Wrong Problem)
> - IF no response after 1 hour: → Go to Step 9 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: problem-match] or [+Add: problem-mismatch]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in qualification-stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

That's exactly what I solve.

**Question 4:** If I could show you a system that lets you lose 30+ pounds in 6 months without missing business meetings or sacrificing your travel schedule, what would that be worth to you?

(My program is $12,000 for 6 months — I need to know you're serious about investing in a real solution)

---

#### STEP 5: Final Qualification + Application Offer

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact shows investment readiness from Question 4

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF shows readiness to invest $12K: → Continue to application
> - IF hesitates on investment: → Go to Step 13 (Investment Education)
> - IF no response after 1 hour: → Go to Step 9 (Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: investment-ready] or [+Add: investment-hesitant]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-ready]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. Based on your answers, you sound like exactly the type of executive I work with.

Here's what I've got for you:

**The Executive Athlete Program** — a 6-month system that gets you in the best shape of your life without leaving your home office.

Dan M. (SaaS CEO) lost 42 pounds while traveling 2 weeks per month. Rick P. (private equity partner) dropped his A1C from 6.2 to 5.4 and lost 28 pounds.

Both said the same thing: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

The program includes:
• Custom training that works in your home office
• Nutrition protocols for business dinners
• Travel systems for hotel gyms
• Weekly video check-ins + private Slack access
• Guarantee: If you don't lose 20 lbs in 90 days following the protocol, I work with you free until you do

Investment: $12,000 for 6 months

I only take 12 clients at a time because of the customization level.

Want to see if you qualify? Fill out this quick application and we'll set up a 15-minute strategy call:

[applicationUrl: DATA NOT PROVIDED — DO NOT INVENT]

Fair enough?

---

#### STEP 6: Application Submitted Follow-up

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact submits application or confirms they've applied

**Time Delay:** Wait 5 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF application received: → Continue with scheduling
> - IF no application after 2 hours: → Go to Step 14 (Application Reminder)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-submitted]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-review]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got your application — thanks for taking the time to fill that out properly.

I'm reviewing it now and will get back to you within 24 hours with next steps.

If you're accepted, we'll schedule a quick 15-minute strategy call to map out your transformation plan.

If you're not quite ready yet, I'll tell you honestly what you need to work on first.

Either way, you'll know where you stand.

Talk soon,
Marcus

P.S. — While you wait, here's a free resource that shows you exactly how to set up a 15-minute home office workout: [COACH: Insert your lead magnet link here]

---

#### STEP 7: Acceptance + Scheduling

**Trigger Condition:**
> Manual trigger after application review (qualified applicant)

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact books call: → Go to Step 15 (Pre-Call Prep)
> - IF no booking after 24 hours: → Go to Step 16 (Booking Reminder)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-accepted]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: call-booking]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Good news — you're exactly the type of executive I love working with.

Your application shows you understand the value of systems and you're serious about solving this problem once and for all.

Let's schedule your 15-minute strategy call where we'll:
• Map out your specific transformation plan
• Show you exactly how The Performance Stack works for your schedule
• Determine if we're a mutual fit

Book your call here: [COACH: Insert your calendar booking link]

I've got spots available this week and next.

Looking forward to talking,
Marcus

---

#### STEP 8: FAQ Handler

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact asks questions instead of answering qualification

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF question about program: → Answer and redirect to qualification
> - IF question about price: → Go to Step 13 (Investment Education)
> - IF question about time commitment: → Answer and redirect

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: has-questions]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in current stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Great question. Let me answer that quickly:

**About the program:** It's designed specifically for executives who travel constantly and work 60+ hour weeks. Everything adapts to your schedule — hotel workouts, business dinner nutrition, jet lag protocols.

**About time commitment:** 15-30 minutes per workout, 4-5x per week. Less time than you spend scrolling social media, but with results that compound for life.

**About results:** Dan lost 42 lbs while traveling 2 weeks per month. Rick reversed prediabetes while running a PE firm. Both in 6 months.

Now, to see if this is right for you, I need to ask those qualification questions.

Let's start with Question 1: What's your current annual revenue?

---

#### STEP 9: Follow-up (No Response)

**Trigger Condition:**
> No response after specified timeframes in previous steps

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 hours from last message

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds: → Return to appropriate step
> - IF no response after 24 hours: → Go to Step 17 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: needs-follow-up]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in current stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey — just following up on my last message.

I know you're busy running your business, but this is exactly why most executives never solve their health problems.

They get distracted by the next fire to put out and keep putting their own well-being on the back burner.

Dan M. was the same way until he realized his declining health was actually hurting his business performance.

If you're serious about getting in the best shape of your life, just reply to my last question and we'll keep moving.

If not, no worries — I get it.

Your call.

---

#### STEP 10: Polite Disqualification (Revenue)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact reports revenue under $300K

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - End sequence after this message

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: revenue-disqualified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: disqualified]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Thanks for your honesty.

Right now I work specifically with executives making $300K+ because they understand the value of investing in high-level systems and have the resources to commit fully.

My recommendation: Focus on growing your revenue first, then come back when you're ready to invest in your health at this level.

In the meantime, grab my free 15-minute workout guide here: [COACH: Insert your lead magnet link]

Best of luck with your business growth.

---

#### STEP 11: Not Ready Yet (Experience)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact hasn't tried multiple fitness approaches

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - End sequence after this message

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: experience-not-ready]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: not-ready]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got it.

It sounds like you haven't hit enough pain points yet to justify investing $12,000 in a solution.

My clients typically come to me after trying 3+ approaches that failed because of their schedule constraints.

My recommendation: Try a few more generic programs first. When you get frustrated enough with the constant restart cycles, come back and we'll talk.

Here's a free resource to get you started: [COACH: Insert your lead magnet link]

---

#### STEP 12: Wrong Problem

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact's main challenge doesn't match what Marcus solves

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - End sequence after this message

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: problem-mismatch]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: wrong-fit]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Thanks for sharing that.

It sounds like your main challenge isn't what I specialize in solving.

I work specifically with executives whose programs fail because of time constraints, travel schedules, and demanding work weeks.

If your issue is different, you might be better served by a different type of coach.

Best of luck finding the right solution.

---

#### STEP 13: Investment Education

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact hesitates about $12K investment

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact shows renewed interest: → Go to Step 5
> - IF still hesitant: → Go to Step 18 (Final Investment Close)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: investment-education-needed]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in current stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I get it — $12,000 feels like a lot.

But let me ask you this: What's it costing you to stay where you are?

• $500+ monthly on unused Peloton/gym memberships
• Lost productivity from low energy by 3pm
• Medical costs from declining health (Rick was prediabetic before working with me)
• The stress on your family watching you struggle

Most executives waste more than $12K per year on solutions that don't work.

This is about solving the problem once instead of managing it forever.

Dan spent years trying cheaper options. None worked until he invested in the right system.

Warren Buffett said the best investment you can make is in yourself. Your health affects everything else you're building.

Still interested in seeing if you qualify?

---

#### STEP 14: Application Reminder

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact expressed interest but didn't submit application

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 hours from Step 5

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF application submitted: → Go to Step 6
> - IF no response after 24 hours: → Go to Step 17 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-reminder-sent]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in current stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Quick reminder — you were interested in The Executive Athlete Program but I don't see your application yet.

I only take 12 clients at a time, and spots fill up fast.

If you're serious about finally solving this problem, fill out the application here: [applicationUrl: DATA NOT PROVIDED — DO NOT INVENT]

If you've changed your mind, just let me know and I'll stop following up.

Your call.

---

#### STEP 15: Pre-Call Prep

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact books strategy call

**Time Delay:** Wait 1 hour after booking

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - Continue to call completion follow-up after call

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: call-booked]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: call-scheduled]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect — I see you booked your call for [CALL TIME].

To make the most of our 15 minutes, please come prepared with:

1. Your current weight and target weight
2. Your typical travel schedule (days per month)
3. Your biggest health concern (energy, strength, appearance, etc.)
4. What you've tried before that didn't work

I'll show you exactly how The Performance Stack would work for your specific situation.

Looking forward to talking,
Marcus

P.S. — The call will be from [COACH: Insert your phone number]. Add it to your contacts so you don't miss it.

---

#### STEP 16: Booking Reminder

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact was accepted but didn't book call within 24 hours

**Time Delay:** Wait 24 hours from Step 7

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF call booked: → Go to Step 15
> - IF no response after 48 hours: → Go to Step 17 (Final Follow-up)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: booking-reminder-sent]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in current stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey — I accepted your application but don't see a call booked yet.

My calendar fills up fast, and I want to make sure you get a spot this week.

Book here: [COACH: Insert your calendar booking link]

If you've decided not to move forward, just let me know so I can offer your spot to someone else.

Thanks,
Marcus

---

#### STEP 17: Final Follow-up

**Trigger Condition:**
> Multiple no-responses or end of sequence

**Time Delay:** Wait 24-48 hours from last message

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - End sequence after this message

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: final-follow-up-sent]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: cold]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Last message from me.

I know you're busy, and I respect that.

If you ever decide you're ready to finally solve your health problems with a system designed for executives who work 60+ hour weeks, you know where to find me.

Until then, here's that free 15-minute workout guide: [COACH: Insert your lead magnet link]

Best of luck with everything.

---

#### STEP 18: Final Investment Close

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact still hesitant after investment education

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact shows interest: → Go to Step 5
> - IF still not ready: → End sequence

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: final-investment-close]
**Pipeline Stage:** Stay in current stage

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I hear you.

Here's the reality: You can keep trying cheaper solutions and hoping something different happens.

Or you can invest in the one system designed specifically for executives like you.

Dan tried everything else first. Wasted 3 years and thousands of dollars. Wishes he'd found me sooner.

Rick's doctor said the program likely saved him [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]+ in future medical costs.

This isn't an expense — it's the highest ROI investment you'll ever make.

But I can't want your transformation more than you do.

If you're ready to stop managing the problem and start solving it, let me know.

If not, I wish you the best with whatever you decide to try next.

Your call.

fitness-sparse.content:
# MANYCHAT AUTOMATION FLOW: THE EXECUTIVE ATHLETE PROGRAM

**DM Keyword:** EXEC

---

## FLOW 1: DM KEYWORD TRIGGER + WELCOME/QUALIFICATION

---
#### NODE: Keyword Response
- **Trigger:** DM keyword "EXEC" or comment "EXEC" on post
- **Delay:** Instant
- **Tags:** [+Add DM-Lead]
- **Custom Fields:** lead_source = dm-keyword

**Message:**
> Hey! Saw you're interested in The Executive Athlete Program.
> 
> Before I share the details, I want to make sure it's actually the right fit for you.
> 
> Quick question - where are you at right now with your health and fitness?

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| Haven't prioritized it in years | Quick Reply | Qualification Q2 |
| Tried multiple approaches | Quick Reply | Qualification Q2 |
| Complete beginner | Quick Reply | Qualification Q2 |

**Next Step:** Routes to Qualification Q2 based on button selection
---

---
#### NODE: Qualification Q2
- **Trigger:** Button tap from previous node
- **Delay:** Instant
- **Tags:** None
- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> Got it. What's your biggest challenge right now?
> 
> Most executives I work with struggle with one of these:

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| No time to train | Quick Reply | Qualification Q3 |
| Programs fall apart when traveling | Quick Reply | Qualification Q3 |
| Trainers don't get my schedule | Quick Reply | Qualification Q3 |
| Can't stay consistent | Quick Reply | Qualification Q3 |

**Next Step:** Routes to Qualification Q3, stores answer in custom field

---

> 
> The Performance Stack is different. It's designed specifically for executives with 60-hour work weeks.
> 
> If this turns out to be the right fit, are you in a position to invest in solving this problem?

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| Yes, ready to invest | Quick Reply | Investment Level Check |
| Need to know more first | Quick Reply | Investment Level Check |
| Not right now | Quick Reply | Disqualification Node |

**Next Step:** Routes based on readiness level

---
#### NODE: Investment Level Check

- **Delay:** Instant
- **Tags:** None

**Message:**

> (This helps me understand if the program investment makes sense for your situation)

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| $500K - $2M | Quick Reply | Qualification Router |
| $2M - $5M | Quick Reply | Qualification Router |
| $5M+ | Quick Reply | Qualification Router |
| Under $500K | Quick Reply | Disqualification Node |

**Next Step:** Routes to qualification router with revenue data

---
#### NODE: Qualification Router

- **Delay:** Instant
- **Tags:** [+Add Qualified] (if qualified) or [+Add Not-Qualified-Yet] (if not ready)
- **Custom Fields:** qualification_status = qualified/nurture

**Message:**
> Perfect. Based on your answers, The Executive Athlete Program could be exactly what you need.

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| Tell me more | Quick Reply | Offer Delivery |

**Next Step:** If qualified (revenue $500K+, ready to invest), routes to Offer Delivery. If "need to know more" was selected earlier, routes to Offer Delivery with nurture tag.
---

## FLOW 2: OFFER DELIVERY + SALES CONVERSION

---
#### NODE: Offer Delivery
- **Trigger:** Button tap from Qualification Router
- **Delay:** Instant
- **Tags:** [+Add Offer-Sent]
- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> Here's what The Executive Athlete Program includes:
> 
> • Custom training system that works in your home office
> • Nutrition protocols for business dinners and travel
> • Weekly video check-ins + biweekly 1-on-1 calls
> • Private Slack access for real-time support

> 
> 6-month program. Investment is $12,000.

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|

---

- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> What's your main question about the program?

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |

| Why is it $12,000? | Quick Reply | Investment Answer |
| What if I can't stick to it? | Quick Reply | Consistency Answer |

#### NODE: Travel Answer

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |

#### NODE: Investment Answer
- **Trigger:** Investment question button
- **Delay:** Instant

> You already invest in business consulting, legal advice, and strategic planning.

> Your health affects every decision you make, every meeting you run, every day with your family.

> $12,000 is less than most executives waste on unused Pelotons and canceled trainers in [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here].

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| That makes sense | URL | [Application Link] |
| Other questions | Quick Reply | Q&A Node |

#### NODE: Consistency Answer

**Message:**

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| I'm ready to start | URL | [Application Link] |
| Other questions | Quick Reply | Q&A Node |

**Next Step:** Application or back to Q&A

---
#### NODE: Social Proof Follow-Up
- **Trigger:** 1 hour delay if no application submitted
- **Delay:** 1 hour
- **Tags:** None
- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> Quick story: Rick P. was a private equity partner with prediabetic A1C of 6.2.
> 
> After 6 months in the program, his A1C dropped to 5.4 and he lost 28 pounds.
> 
> His doctor couldn't believe the numbers.
> 
> Ready to get started?

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| Apply now | URL | [Application Link] |
| Not ready yet | Quick Reply | Nurture Sequence |

**Next Step:** Application or nurture sequence

---
#### NODE: Urgency Reminder
- **Trigger:** 24 hours if no application
- **Delay:** 24 hours
- **Tags:** None
- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> I only work with 12 active clients at a time.

> The best time to start is when you're feeling this pull to change.
> 
> Don't let another quarter go by watching your health decline.

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| Apply now | URL | [Application Link] |
| Not ready | Quick Reply | Nurture Sequence |

**Next Step:** Application or nurture sequence
---

---
#### NODE: Final Conversion Push
- **Trigger:** 48 hours if still no action
- **Delay:** 48 hours
- **Tags:** None
- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> Picture this: Walking into every boardroom with the confidence of someone who owns their physical presence.
> 
> Energy that lasts from 6am to 8pm. Taking your shirt off at the beach without thinking twice.
> 
> You've built an empire. Now build the body that matches it.
> 
> If this resonates, here's the link. If not, no worries at all.

**Buttons:**
| Button Label | Action | Connects To |
|---|---|---|
| I'm ready | URL | [Application Link] |

**Next Step:** This is the final sales message. No further follow-up.

> Your constraint isn't time - it's efficiency.

> But you don't run your business on willpower. You have systems.
> 
> Your health needs the same systematic approach.

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |

#### NODE: Nurture Day 3
- **Trigger:** 24 hours after Day 2

> 
> He kept trying harder instead of trying smarter.
> 
> Once he got the right system, he lost 42 lbs while traveling 2 weeks per month.

---
#### NODE: Nurture Day 4
- **Trigger:** 24 hours after Day 3
- **Delay:** 24 hours
- **Tags:** None
- **Custom Fields:** None

**Message:**
> Another thing executives get wrong:
>

> [COACH: Insert link to your lead magnet]

---
#### NODE: Nurture Day 6
- **Trigger:** 24 hours after Day 5

> 
> "I should focus my time on business and family. Working out is selfish."

> Your health is the foundation that makes you better at everything else.

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |

**Message:**

>

| Button Label | Action | Connects To |

## DISQUALIFICATION NODE

#### NODE: Polite Redirect

## MANYCHAT SETUP REQUIREMENTS

- If subscriber has "Nurture-Complete" tag → Move to re-engagement list after [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here]

fitness-sparse.content:
# Facebook Posts for Marcus Reed — Reed Performance

---

## Post 1 (VALUE POST)
**Type:** Value | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

Most executives think they need 90 minutes in a gym to see real results.

Here's what actually works:

20 minutes of focused training beats 90 minutes of distracted gym time every single time.

The secret isn't duration — it's intensity and consistency.

Your body adapts to stimulus, not time spent. A targeted home office session that hits all movement patterns will build more strength than an hour of wandering around a crowded gym.

Try this today: Set a 15-minute timer. Do 3 rounds of bodyweight squats, push-ups, and planks. Focus completely for those 15 minutes.

You'll get more done than most people do in an hour.

What's been your experience with shorter, focused workouts versus long gym sessions?

---

## Post 2 (STORY POST)
**Type:** Story | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Executives who've engaged with fitness content

In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even.

I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting the clients I was supposed to help.

The irony wasn't lost on me — I was in the fitness industry but couldn't find time for my own health.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, thinking it was temporary.

That Zoom session changed everything.

I realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. Better yet, the constraints of remote coaching forced me to create better systems.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online.

Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs that actually fit their reality.

Sometimes the best solutions come from the worst situations.

The lesson: Don't let perfect be the enemy of good. Work with your constraints, not against them.

---

## Post 3 (BELIEF-SHIFTING POST)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Business owners making 300K+

You wouldn't run your business on willpower alone.

You have systems, processes, and accountability measures that ensure things get done regardless of how motivated you feel on any given day.

Your health deserves the same systematic approach.

Yet most executives rely on motivation for fitness — then wonder why they keep starting and stopping.

Here's the truth: Stanford researchers proved that systems beat willpower every time. The executives who transform their bodies don't have more discipline. They have better systems.

Stop trying harder. Start building better systems.

Your business runs on processes that work when you don't feel like it. Your body needs the same approach.

What systems do you have in your business that could apply to your health?

---

## Post 4 (VALUE POST)
**Type:** Value | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

The hotel room workout that keeps executives on track:

No gym. No equipment. No excuses.

Round 1:
- 10 bodyweight squats
- 10 push-ups (modify on knees if needed)
- 30-second plank

Round 2:
- 15 bodyweight squats
- 15 push-ups
- 45-second plank

Round 3:
- 20 bodyweight squats
- 20 push-ups
- 60-second plank

Total time: 12 minutes.

This hits every major muscle group and gets your heart rate up. You can do it in a 6x6 space wearing business clothes.

The key isn't having perfect conditions. The key is having a system that works in any condition.

Try this on your next business trip: 12 minutes before your first meeting.

What's your go-to travel workout?

---

## Post 5 (RESULTS/PROOF POST)
**Type:** Results | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - People who've viewed program content

Dan M. was a SaaS CEO, 42 pounds overweight, hadn't trained consistently in 3 years.

His biggest complaint: "No trainer understands what running a business is actually like."

He'd tried Peloton, local trainers, multiple apps. Nothing stuck because nothing fit his reality.

Here's what we did differently:

- Built workouts around his actual schedule, not ideal conditions
- Created nutrition protocols that worked during client dinners
- Developed travel routines for his 2-week monthly trips

Result: 42 pounds lost in 6 months. Now squats 2x his bodyweight and maintains his results while traveling constantly.

His words: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

The difference wasn't motivation. The difference was a system designed for executives, not for people with unlimited time.

When you solve the right problem, results become inevitable.

---

## Post 6 (CTA POST)
**Type:** CTA | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Executives who've engaged multiple times

There are two types of male executives:

Those who keep saying "I'll start Monday" and stay exactly where they are.

And those who become The Executive Athlete — high performers who take their body as seriously as their P&L.

The Executive Athlete Program is for executives who are done making excuses.

This is for you if:
- You run a company making $500K+ and understand systems
- You've tried 3+ approaches that failed because of your schedule  
- You're willing to invest $12,000 to solve this problem permanently
- You want a trainer who actually understands what running a business is like

This is NOT for you if:
- You want a quick fix or magic solution
- You're not willing to follow a structured protocol for 6 months
- You're looking for the cheapest option available

I only work with 12 clients at a time. When those spots are filled, there's a waiting list.

DM me "ATHLETE" if you're ready to stop talking and start transforming.

---

## Post 7 (BELIEF-SHIFTING POST)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - High-income business owners

$12,000 over 6 months is $67 per day.

You spend more than that on business lunches that sabotage your health.

Yet you call fitness coaching "expensive."

Here's the math that matters:

- Peloton: $500/month forever, zero results
- Local trainers: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]/session, constant cancellations
- Generic programs: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]/month, doesn't work with travel

Total wasted: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]+ annually on solutions that don't work.

Or: $12,000 once for a system that solves the problem permanently.

You already know this from business. Premium pricing produces premium results. Cheap solutions create cheap transformations.

You invest [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]in business consultants but won't invest $12,000 in the foundation that makes everything else possible.

Your body is your most important business asset.

What's the real cost of treating it like a liability?

---

## Post 8 (VALUE POST)
**Type:** Value | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

Why business travel destroys your fitness routine:

You're planning for everything except your health.

Here's the executive travel protocol:

**Before you leave:**
- Pack resistance bands (takes 2 minutes, weighs nothing)
- Download hotel gym photos (most have basic equipment)
- Plan one bodyweight backup routine

**During travel:**
- 15 minutes in your room beats 0 minutes looking for a gym
- Protein at every meal, especially breakfast
- Walk during phone calls instead of sitting

**When you return:**
- Don't restart — continue where you left off
- One missed day doesn't require a complete reset

The goal isn't perfection. The goal is consistency within your actual constraints.

Most executives fail because they plan for ideal conditions that never exist.

What's your biggest challenge with staying consistent during travel?

---

## Post 9 (STORY POST)
**Type:** Story | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Executives struggling with consistency

A client asked me: "How do I stay motivated when work gets crazy?"

Wrong question.

The right question: "How do I build a system that works when motivation fails?"

Motivation is like the weather — unpredictable and outside your control.

Systems are like your business processes — they work whether you feel like it or not.

You don't rely on feeling motivated to make payroll. You have systems that ensure it happens.

You don't wait to feel inspired to serve clients. You have processes that deliver results consistently.

Your health needs the same approach.

The executives who transform their bodies don't have more willpower. They have better systems.

Stop chasing motivation. Start building systems.

What's one system in your business that works automatically?

---

## Post 10 (IDENTITY POST)
**Type:** Identity | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Business owners 40-55

You're either becoming The Executive Athlete or staying The Peloton Paperweight Executive.

**The Peloton Paperweight Executive:**
- Catches his reflection in elevator mirrors and feels disappointed
- Exhausted by 3pm every day
- Dreads his annual physical
- Owns expensive equipment that collects dust
- Makes excuses about his schedule

**The Executive Athlete:**
- Walks into every room with quiet confidence
- Has energy that lasts from 6am to 8pm
- Takes his shirt off at the beach without thinking twice
- Travels 2 weeks per month and stays on track
- Takes his body as seriously as his P&L

Same person. Different identity.

The choice you make today determines which one you become tomorrow.

Every month you delay is another month as The Peloton Paperweight Executive.

Every month you act is another month closer to The Executive Athlete.

Which identity are you choosing?

---

## Post 11 (RESULTS/PROOF POST)
**Type:** Results | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - People interested in health transformation

Rick P. was a private equity partner with a prediabetic A1C of 6.2.

His doctor was concerned. His wife was worried. He was heading down the same path as his father.

The problem wasn't knowledge — he knew he needed to change.

The problem was no system that worked with client dinners, unpredictable schedules, and constant pressure.

Here's what happened in 6 months:
- A1C dropped from 6.2 to 5.4
- Lost 28 pounds
- Energy levels sustained throughout 12-hour days
- Doctor amazed by the transformation

His words: "My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

The difference wasn't willpower. The difference was a nutrition and training system designed for executives who can't meal prep every Sunday.

When you solve the right problem, your body responds fast.

---

## Post 12 (BELIEF-SHIFTING POST)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Executives who think they don't have time

You don't have a time problem. You have a priority problem.

Two executives, identical schedules:

Executive A: "I don't have time to work out."
- Spends 30 minutes scrolling social media after dinner
- Watches Netflix for an hour before bed
- Complains about having no energy

Executive B: Uses those same 30 minutes for targeted training.
- More energy throughout the day
- Better sleep at night
- Stronger and leaner every month

Same 24 hours. Different choices.

You're not too busy to get in shape. You're too busy doing things that don't matter.

The real problem isn't time scarcity. The real problem is priority confusion.

What would change if you treated your health like your most important business meeting?

---

## Post 13 (VALUE POST)
**Type:** Value | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

The 15-minute morning session that changes everything:

Round 1 (5 minutes):
- 1 minute bodyweight squats
- 1 minute push-ups
- 1 minute plank
- 2 minutes rest

Round 2 (5 minutes):
- 1 minute jumping jacks
- 1 minute mountain climbers  
- 1 minute burpees
- 2 minutes rest

Round 3 (5 minutes):
- 1 minute wall sit
- 1 minute high knees
- 1 minute bicycle crunches
- 2 minutes cooldown

This hits strength, cardio, and core. Gets your heart rate up and your energy flowing.

Do this before your first call and you'll have more energy at 3pm than most people have at 9am.

The key: consistency beats intensity. 15 minutes daily beats 2 hours weekly.

What time could you consistently do 15 minutes of movement?

---

## Post 14 (POLARIZING/FILTER POST)
**Type:** Polarizing | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** N

I refuse to work with executives who blame their schedule for their choices.

"I don't have time" is code for "this isn't a priority."

You find time for what matters. You make time for what's urgent.

Your health should be both.

If you're looking for a coach who will coddle you and accept your excuses, keep scrolling.

If you want someone who will hold you to the same standard you hold your employees, we should talk.

I work with executives who are tired of making excuses and ready to apply the same discipline that built their business to building their body.

Everyone else can unfollow.

The Executive Athlete Program isn't for people who want to be comfortable. It's for people who want to be transformed.

Which one are you?

---

## Post 15 (CTA POST)
**Type:** CTA | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Warm audience who's engaged with multiple posts

Every month you catch your reflection in that elevator mirror is a choice.

Accept mediocrity or demand excellence.

Stay The Peloton Paperweight Executive or become The Executive Athlete.

Keep making the same excuses or start building the same systems that transformed Dan M. and Rick P.

The Executive Athlete Program opens for 12 new clients this month.

The application process:
1. Brief form (5 minutes)
2. Strategy call (30 minutes) 
3. Mutual fit assessment
4. Program customization
5. Transformation begins

Not everyone is accepted. This is for serious executives only.

If you're ready to stop talking and start transforming, DM me "READY" and I'll send you the application.

If you're not ready, that's fine. But stop complaining about your reflection.

The choice is yours.

## **AD 1: Problem-Agitation (Cold Traffic)**

### Primary Text (Short)
**Most CEOs spend more time maintaining their car than their body. Then wonder why they're exhausted by 3pm.**

### Primary Text (Long)
**Most CEOs spend more time maintaining their car than their body. Then wonder why they're exhausted by 3pm.**

Here's what I see with executives making $300K+:

They'll hire the best accountants, lawyers, and consultants... but trust their health to whatever trainer has an opening at 6am.

They'll track every KPI in their business but have no clue what their resting heart rate is.

Listen — I get it. I spent 11 years training executives who thought they could outsmart biology with willpower.

Spoiler alert: You can't.

Your body doesn't care about your Q4 numbers. It doesn't care that you "don't have time." It's keeping score whether you are or not.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

The difference? I don't give executives cookie-cutter programs. I build systems that work with your reality, not against it.

**Because your schedule isn't the problem. Generic solutions are.**

You guys always tell me the same thing: "Marcus, I don't have time to train."

Bullshit.

You have time. You just don't have a system that works with your schedule instead of against it.

Most trainers give executives hour-long workouts designed for college kids with nothing but time.

Here's what works: 15 minutes. Home office. No equipment except your own bodyweight.

I've taken this exact workout and given it to 340 clients over 11 years. CEOs, founders, executives running companies from $500K to $5M.

The ones who don't? They're still making excuses about "starting after this quarter ends."

**Download The Executive's 15-Minute Workout and stop leaving your health to chance.**

### Primary Text (Long)
**Your Peloton isn't the problem. Your approach is.**

I see this pattern with every executive who calls me:

They buy the [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here] Peloton. Use it twice. Then it becomes the world's most expensive clothing rack.

Generic programs fail because they're built for people with predictable schedules. You're not most people.

You need protocols for hotel gyms. Nutrition strategies for client dinners. Recovery systems for jet lag.

You need The Performance Stack — training that syncs to your business rhythm, not some trainer's fantasy schedule.

fitness-sparse.content:
# YouTube Video Script: The Executive Athlete Transformation

**Video Title:** "Why Willpower Fails Executives (And The 3-Layer System That Actually Works)"

**Thumbnail Text:** "WILLPOWER vs SYSTEMS"

**Video Description:** 
Male executives making $300K+ struggle with fitness because they're using the wrong approach. In this video, I break down why willpower-based fitness fails and reveal The Performance Stack — the 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to your 60-hour work week. Learn how Dan M. lost 42 pounds while traveling 2 weeks per month and why systems beat motivation every time.

**Core Belief Shift:** From "I just need more willpower to stick to a fitness program" to "Success in fitness, like business, comes from having the right system."

**Target Word Count:** 2,200 words (15-minute video)

---

## 1. HOOK (0:00-0:30) [120 words]

Listen up — if you're a male executive making serious money but you're 30-40 pounds overweight, exhausted by 3pm, and catching your reflection in elevator mirrors thinking "what the hell happened to me," this video is going to change everything.

[ON SCREEN: "For Executives Making $300K+ Who Are Tired of Failed Fitness Attempts"]

I'm going to show you why every fitness program you've tried has failed, and it's not because you lack willpower. In fact, if you're successful in business, you've already proven you have more discipline than 99% of people. The problem is you're using a 1995 fitness model in a 2024 executive lifestyle.

By the end of this video, you'll understand why systems beat willpower every single time, and I'll give you the exact 3-layer system my clients use to lose 30+ pounds without missing a single business meeting.

## 2. INTRO (0:30-1:30) [150 words]

My name is Marcus Reed. I've coached 340 executives over 11 years, and I help busy executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

Here's what you're going to learn today: First, why willpower-based fitness is designed to fail for people like you. Second, the real reason you've been stuck for 3-5 years despite trying everything. And third, The Performance Stack — the proven system that works with your schedule, not against it.

[ON SCREEN: "Marcus Reed - 11 Years, 340 Clients Coached"]

This is for male executives age 40-55 who run their own companies and haven't prioritized their health in years. If you're looking for a magic pill or overnight transformation, this isn't for you. But if you're ready to apply the same systematic thinking that built your business to building your body, keep watching.

Right now, hit subscribe if you're serious about becoming the executive athlete you're meant to be.

## 3. PROBLEM (1:30-4:00) [375 words]

Here's what I need you to understand: You probably think your problem is willpower. "If I can build a million-dollar business, I should be able to stick to a workout plan, right?" So you download MyFitnessPal, promise yourself you'll hit the gym at 6am, and rely on motivation to carry you through.

[ON SCREEN: "The Willpower Trap"]

But here's what happens. Week one goes great. You're motivated, you're consistent, you feel like you've finally figured it out. Week two, a client emergency hits. You skip one workout. Week three, you're traveling for business. The nutrition plan falls apart during client dinners. By week four, you're back to square one, feeling like a failure.

Sound familiar?

You catch your reflection in that elevator mirror each morning and feel that familiar stab of shame. You're exhausted by 3pm when you used to have energy all day. You're dreading your next physical because you know the numbers are getting worse. You've got a $3,000 Peloton collecting dust while you waste $500 monthly on trainers you keep canceling.

[ON SCREEN: "The Real Cost of Failed Attempts"]

And here's the brutal truth — every month you stay stuck is another month closer to the same heart attack that took your father at 58. You're watching your younger employees outlift you at company retreats. You're skipping your son's baseball games because you're too exhausted to show up.

The willpower approach has you trapped in what I call the "restart cycle." You start strong, life gets in the way, you abandon the program, you feel guilty, you start again with even more determination. Rinse and repeat.

But here's what's really happening: You're not failing because you lack discipline. You're failing because you're using a system designed for people with unlimited time and predictable schedules. You're trying to force a consumer-grade solution into an enterprise-level problem.

You wouldn't run your business on willpower alone, right? You don't rely on feeling motivated to make payroll or serve clients. You have systems, processes, and accountability measures that ensure things get done regardless of how you feel on any given day.

So why would you treat your health any differently?

## 4. NEW BELIEF / PARADIGM SHIFT (4:00-8:00) [600 words]

Here's the truth that's going to change everything for you: Success in fitness, like business, comes from having the right system. The Performance Stack is the proven system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

[ON SCREEN: "Systems Beat Willpower Every Time"]

Let me prove this to you.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Authority Reference]**
James Clear shows in "Atomic Habits" that environment beats willpower every single time. He proves that if you rely on systems instead of willpower, you'll go from inconsistent failure to automatic success. The most successful people don't rely on motivation — they build systems that make the right choices inevitable.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Universal Analogy]**
Think about it this way: You wouldn't try to run your business without systems. You have processes for everything — sales, operations, customer service. You don't wake up each morning hoping you'll feel motivated enough to serve your clients. You have systems that ensure it happens regardless of your mood.

Your health deserves the same systematic approach.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Cross-Domain Reference]**
Look at any high-performing organization. They don't rely on their employees being motivated every day. They build systems that create predictable outcomes. McKinsey doesn't hope their consultants will feel like doing good work — they have frameworks and processes that ensure quality regardless of individual motivation.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Client Story]**
Dan M. is a SaaS CEO who was exactly where you are. Burnt out, 42 pounds overweight, hadn't trained consistently in 3 years. He kept trying to force himself to stick to generic programs, but his schedule was chaos. He travels 2 weeks per month, works 60-hour weeks, has client dinners constantly.

The breakthrough came when he stopped trying to be more disciplined and started following The Performance Stack. He lost 42 pounds in 6 months and now maintains his results while traveling constantly. As Dan told me: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

**[PROOF BLOCK: Logic Trap]**
Here's the thing — if willpower worked for busy executives, every successful CEO would be in peak physical shape. But look around. Most executives making serious money are out of shape despite having more discipline than 99% of the population. This proves that willpower isn't the answer.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Personal Story]**
I learned this the hard way. In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients. I was in the fitness industry but couldn't maintain my own fitness because I didn't have systems.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, and realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. But more importantly, the constraints of remote coaching forced me to create better systems. I had to design programs that worked in home offices, hotel rooms, around unpredictable schedules.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. That's when I developed The Performance Stack — a system that works with executive constraints, not against them.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Statistics/Data]**
Here's the proof: Out of my last 60 clients, 52 achieved their transformation by following the system. That's an 87% success rate. These aren't people with unlimited time — they're executives running companies, traveling constantly, working 60-hour weeks.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Reframe]**
What if the problem isn't that you don't have enough willpower? What if the problem is that you've been trying to use the wrong tool for the job? You wouldn't use a screwdriver to hammer a nail. Stop trying to use willpower to solve a systems problem.

The executives who transform aren't more disciplined than you. They just have better systems.

## 5. PROOF / STORIES (8:00-12:00) [600 words]

Let me tell you about Rick P. — a private equity partner who was convinced his problem was willpower. He had a prediabetic A1C of 6.2, was 28 pounds overweight, and kept starting and stopping fitness programs.

[ON SCREEN: "Rick P. - Private Equity Partner"]

Rick would start strong every Monday. He'd meal prep on Sunday, plan his workouts, promise himself this time would be different. But by Wednesday, he'd have a client dinner that destroyed his nutrition plan. By Friday, he'd miss a workout due to a deal closing. By the following Monday, he'd abandoned the program entirely.

Sound familiar?

Rick thought he needed more discipline. But here's what was really happening: He was using a system designed for people with predictable schedules and no business responsibilities. The system was the problem, not his willpower.

When Rick started The Performance Stack, everything changed. Instead of meal prepping elaborate meals that fell apart during business travel, he learned nutrition strategies that work during client entertainment. Instead of trying to hit the gym at specific times, he got workouts designed for hotel rooms and home offices.

Six months later, Rick's A1C dropped from 6.2 to 5.4. He lost 28 pounds. His doctor couldn't believe the numbers. And here's the key — his business didn't suffer. In fact, his energy and focus improved so much that his performance actually enhanced.

[ON SCREEN: "A1C: 6.2 → 5.4, Lost 28 lbs, Doctor Amazed"]

As Rick told me: "My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

But this isn't just about individual willpower versus systems. Let me show you how this plays out in the real world.

Think about two executives — both successful, both busy, both struggling with their health. Executive A relies on willpower. He downloads fitness apps, buys equipment, promises himself he'll be more disciplined. He starts strong but fails when life gets chaotic.

Executive B uses systems. He has a program that adapts to his schedule, nutrition protocols that work during business travel, accountability that fits his leadership style. When life gets chaotic, his system adapts with him.

Which executive do you think succeeds long-term?

[ON SCREEN: "Willpower vs Systems"]

Here's what I've learned from coaching 340 executives: The ones who transform aren't more motivated than the ones who stay stuck. They just have better systems.

Stanford researchers proved this. They studied habit formation and found that if you rely on systems instead of willpower, you go from inconsistent failure to automatic success. The specific mechanism is environmental design — when you build the right environment, the right choices become inevitable.

This is exactly what The Performance Stack does. It creates an environment where consistency becomes automatic, not something you have to force through willpower.

When I was running my gym, I saw this pattern constantly. The clients who succeeded weren't the most motivated ones. They were the ones who had systems that worked with their real life, not against it.

The executives who failed? They kept trying to force themselves into programs designed for people with unlimited time. They'd show up motivated on Monday and burn out by Thursday because they were fighting their reality instead of working with it.

That's when I realized: executives don't need more willpower. They need systems designed for their actual constraints.

## 6. ACTION STEPS (12:00-15:00) [450 words]

Alright, here's how you apply this starting today. I'm going to give you the three layers of The Performance Stack so you can begin building your own system.

[ON SCREEN: "The Performance Stack - 3 Layers"]

**Layer 1: Training That Adapts**
Stop trying to force your schedule around fitness. Instead, design fitness around your schedule. Here's what you do: Block out three 20-minute windows this week. Not at specific times — just three windows that fit your reality. Maybe it's 6am Tuesday, 2pm Thursday, 8am Saturday. Doesn't matter when, just block the time.

For those 20 minutes, you're doing bodyweight exercises that require zero equipment. Push-ups, squats, planks. Simple movements that work whether you're in your home office or a hotel room. The goal isn't perfection — it's consistency with your constraints.

**Layer 2: Nutrition That Travels**
Forget meal prepping elaborate meals that fall apart during business travel. Instead, learn the principles that work anywhere. Here's your first principle: protein at every meal. Whether you're at a client dinner or grabbing airport food, prioritize protein. This one change stabilizes your energy and controls hunger without requiring perfect meal prep.

Track this for one week. Don't worry about calories or macros yet. Just focus on getting protein at every meal. You'll be amazed how this one systematic change affects your energy and cravings.

**Layer 3: Recovery That Enhances Performance**
Most executives think recovery is for athletes. Wrong. Recovery is for anyone who wants to perform at their peak. Here's your recovery protocol: seven hours of sleep minimum, and a 10-minute wind-down routine before bed.

No phones, no emails, no business calls in that final 10 minutes. This isn't about relaxation — it's about optimizing your most important business asset: your brain.

[ON SCREEN: "Start With These 3 Changes This Week"]

Here's what to expect: Week one, you'll notice better energy and sleep quality. Week two, you'll start seeing strength gains from consistent movement. Week three, your clothes will fit differently because you're building muscle and losing fat simultaneously.

The key is tracking your leading indicators, not just the scale. Energy levels, sleep quality, how your clothes fit — these tell you the system is working before the weight comes off.

Remember, you're not trying to be perfect. You're building a system that works with your reality. When you travel, the system travels with you. When work gets crazy, the system adapts. When motivation fails, the system keeps you moving forward.

This is how you become the executive athlete — not through willpower, but through systems that make the right choices automatic.

## 7. CTA (15:00-End) [200 words]

So remember — it's not about having more willpower. It's about having the right system. The Performance Stack syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of your 60-hour work week.

[ON SCREEN: "Systems Beat Willpower Every Time"]

Imagine walking into every boardroom with the confidence of someone who owns their physical presence. Your energy carries you through 12-hour days and still leaves fuel in the tank for family time. You take your shirt off at the beach without thinking twice. You've become the executive athlete — a high performer who takes his body as seriously as his P&L.

If you want the complete Performance Stack system, I've created a free guide called "The Executive's 15-Minute Workout." It's the exact bodyweight routine my clients use when they're traveling or pressed for time. Download it using the link in the description.

[ON SCREEN: "Free Download: The Executive's 15-Minute Workout"]

For executives ready to go deeper, I have 12 spots available in The Executive Athlete Program. This is where we build your complete transformation system over 6 months. Application link is also in the description.

Hit subscribe and the bell if this helped you understand why systems beat willpower. And drop a comment below — what's your biggest takeaway from this video?

Thanks for watching, and I'll see you in the next one.

[ON SCREEN: "Subscribe + Bell for More Executive Fitness Content"]

# Short-Form Video Scripts: The Executive Athlete System

---

## Short #1 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Pattern Interrupt Hook  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok  
**Duration:** 30 seconds

### Hook
"If you're making $300K+ but can't stick to a workout plan..."

### Body Script
If you're making $300K+ but can't stick to a workout plan, the problem isn't your willpower. You built a business. You have more discipline than 99% of people. The problem? You're using a 1995 fitness model in a 2024 executive lifestyle.

You wouldn't run your business on motivation alone, right? You don't hope you feel like making payroll. You have systems that ensure it gets done regardless of how you feel.

So why treat your health differently? Systems beat willpower every single time.

### On-Screen Text
- "Making $300K+ but can't stick to fitness?"
- "It's not willpower"
- "You're using the wrong system"
- "Systems > Willpower"

### CTA/Closing
"Follow for the system that actually works for executives."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with quick cuts between points. B-roll of executive in suit catching reflection in elevator mirror.

### Hashtags
#ExecutiveFitness #SystemsNotWillpower #BusyExecutive #FitnessForCEOs #PerformanceCoaching

---

## Short #2 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Old Way vs New Way  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok  
**Duration:** 45 seconds

### Hook
"Why every fitness program fails busy executives..."

### Body Script
Here's why every fitness program fails busy executives. You download MyFitnessPal, promise to hit the gym at 6am, rely on motivation to carry you through.

Week one goes great. Week two, client emergency. You skip one workout. Week three, business travel. Nutrition plan falls apart during client dinners. Week four, you're back to square one feeling like a failure.

This is the restart cycle. And it's not your fault. You're using consumer-grade solutions for enterprise-level problems. The executives who transform don't have more willpower. They have systems designed for 60-hour work weeks.

### On-Screen Text
- "Why fitness programs fail executives"
- "Week 1: Motivated"
- "Week 2: Client emergency"
- "Week 3: Business travel"
- "Week 4: Back to square one"
- "Consumer solutions ≠ Executive problems"

### CTA/Closing
"Want the system that works? Link in bio."

### Suggested Visual
Split screen showing "typical program" vs "executive reality" with calendar overlays showing conflicts.

### Hashtags
#ExecutiveFitness #FitnessMyths #BusyExecutive #SystemsThinking #CEOHealth

---

## Short #3 — Story Clip
**Type:** Personal Transformation  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok  
**Duration:** 50 seconds

### Hook
"I was training 12 hours a day and still gaining weight..."

### Body Script
In 2021, I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting my clients. I was in the fitness industry but couldn't maintain my own fitness.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, and everything changed. I realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. But more importantly, remote coaching forced me to create better systems.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. That's when I developed The Performance Stack — a system that works with executive constraints, not against them. 340 clients later, I've proven systems beat willpower every time.

### On-Screen Text
- "Training 12 hrs/day but gaining weight"
- "Burnt out in the fitness industry"
- "COVID changed everything"
- "Zoom training = better systems"
- "Closed gym, went online"
- "340 clients transformed"

### CTA/Closing
"Ready for your system? DM me 'STACK'"

### Suggested Visual
Before/after style with gym footage transitioning to home office setup. Show client transformation photos.

### Hashtags
#PersonalTrainerStory #COVID #SystemsOverWillpower #OnlineCoaching #ExecutiveFitness

---

## Short #4 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Reframe the Problem  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok  
**Duration:** 35 seconds

### Hook
"You think your problem is willpower..."

### Body Script
You think your problem is willpower. But here's the truth: If willpower worked for busy executives, every successful CEO would be in peak shape. Look around. Most executives making serious money are out of shape despite having more discipline than 99% of people.

This proves willpower isn't the answer. What if the problem isn't that you don't have enough discipline? What if you've been trying to use a screwdriver to hammer a nail? Stop using willpower to solve a systems problem.

### On-Screen Text
- "Think it's willpower?"
- "If willpower worked..."
- "Every CEO would be fit"
- "Most aren't"
- "Willpower ≠ Answer"
- "Wrong tool for the job"

### CTA/Closing
"Follow for the right tool."

### Suggested Visual
Montage of successful but out-of-shape executives, then transition to fit executive in boardroom.

### Hashtags
#WillpowerMyth #ExecutiveReality #SystemsThinking #CEOFitness #BusyExecutive

---

## Short #5 — Case Study Clip
**Type:** Client Transformation  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok  
**Duration:** 40 seconds

### Hook
"SaaS CEO, 42 pounds overweight, travels 2 weeks per month..."

### Body Script
Dan M. is a SaaS CEO. 42 pounds overweight, burnt out, hadn't trained consistently in 3 years. He travels 2 weeks per month, works 60-hour weeks, constant client dinners.

He kept trying to force himself into generic programs, but his schedule was chaos. The breakthrough came when he stopped trying to be more disciplined and started following The Performance Stack.

Six months later: lost 42 pounds, squats 2x bodyweight, maintains results while traveling constantly. As Dan told me: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Your [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]Peloton is collecting dust. You're wasting $500 monthly on trainers you keep canceling. You catch your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning and feel that familiar stab of shame.

business-abstract.title: Mission Statement
business-abstract.content:
# MISSION STATEMENT FOR SARAH CHEN

**WE WILL BUILD A SIGNATURE GROUP PROGRAM WITH 100 MEMBERS BY [COACH: INSERT YOUR DEADLINE HERE] BECAUSE THE HUSTLE PLAYBOOK IS KILLING A GENERATION OF FEMALE FOUNDERS AND NO ONE IS TELLING THEM THE TRUTH ABOUT WHY. THEY WILL NO LONGER BE RESENTFUL, EXHAUSTED, INVISIBLE, AND LONELY AT THE TOP, AND WILL INSTEAD BECOME SPACIOUS, GROUNDED, SELF-TRUSTING, AND PROUD SOVEREIGN CEOS WHO LEAD FROM FULLNESS.**

---

## THE BREAKDOWN

**THE GOAL** — We're building a signature group program with 100 members. Not just any program — one that transforms six-figure female founders who are trapped by their own success. This isn't about scaling for scaling's sake. It's about reaching the women who are silently drowning in their "successful" businesses.

**THE DEADLINE** — [COACH: INSERT YOUR SPECIFIC DEADLINE HERE] Because every day we delay, another brilliant woman is working 70-hour weeks, drinking to sleep, and resenting the business she built. Urgency isn't about pressure — it's about impact. These women can't wait for us to figure it out.

**THE REASON** — The hustle playbook is literally killing a generation of female founders. They're hitting income ceilings not because they lack strategy, but because their self-worth is tied to productivity. They're building businesses on crumbling inner foundations, and nobody is telling them the truth about why their tactics aren't working. We're not just coaching — we're intervening in a crisis.

**THE TRANSFORMATION** — Every woman we help stops being a resentful high-achiever who can't stop and becomes a sovereign CEO who leads from fullness. She goes from exhausted and invisible to spacious and self-trusting. From working 70-hour weeks to running her business from self-trust instead of self-abandonment. From charging half of what she's worth to tripling her prices without guilt.

---

## DAILY REMINDER VERSION

**We're building 100 members because the hustle playbook is killing brilliant women, and we're the ones telling them the truth about why.**

---

## TEAM / PUBLIC VERSION

We help female founders rewrite the inner narratives that keep them stuck below six figures. Our mission is to build a signature group program with 100 members because the hustle playbook is killing a generation of brilliant women, and someone needs to tell them the truth about why their tactics aren't working. We transform resentful high-achievers into sovereign CEOs who lead from fullness, not self-abandonment.

business-abstract.title: Human Bio

scannerHits.evidence: When Sarah Chen isn't helping female founders rewrite t

business-abstract.content:
When Sarah Chen isn't helping female founders rewrite the inner narratives keeping them stuck, you'll find her at the pottery wheel, hands deep in clay, or pulling tarot cards to process whatever uncomfortable truth she's been sitting with that week.

Born in Taiwan and raised in Canada from age 8, Sarah learned early that achievement equaled worthiness — a lesson her parents drilled in with the best of intentions. She carried that programming straight into a successful product management role at Shopify, then into building a consulting business that hit $420K in 3 years. On paper, everything looked perfect. In reality, she was working 70-hour weeks and drinking herself to sleep.

Sarah lives in Toronto with her partner and a border collie who's probably the only one who can get her to take actual breaks. When she's not hiking the trails around the city, she's usually found somewhere quiet with her hands in dirt — whether that's her pottery studio or tending to plants that don't demand anything from her except presence.

What drives her beyond the business is simple: the hustle playbook is killing a generation of female founders, and no one is telling them the truth about why. After spending 2 years in her own deep inner work and getting ICF-certified, she pivoted everything to coach women through the exact patterns that had kept her trapped.

She's the kind of person who'll name the thing everyone's thinking but no one wants to say — and somehow make you feel less alone for thinking it too.

business-abstract.content:
# Magnetic Messaging Map for Sarah Chen

## 1. THE 10-ELEMENT MAP

**1. Skills:** IFS parts work, somatic regulation, nervous system rewiring, pricing psychology, identity-level transformation for high-achieving women

**2. Problems:** Six-figure female founders hitting income ceilings because they're running their business from self-abandonment instead of self-trust, trapped in 70-hour weeks while undercharging

**3. Ideal Audience:** Women founders age 30-45 running service businesses making $150K-$500K who feel trapped by their own success

**4. Complaints:** No time for anything but client work, can't charge what she's worth, working Sundays again, 47 unread Slack messages at 10pm

**5. Goals:** Permission to slow down, raise prices without guilt, take a month off without the business collapsing, run a business from fullness not depletion

**6. Unwanted Feelings:** Resentful, exhausted, invisible, lonely at the top

**7. Desired Feelings:** Spacious, grounded, self-trusting, proud

**8. Old Way:** Reading more business books, joining masterminds, following mindset coaches on Instagram, journaling sporadically, trying to be more disciplined

**9. Your Way:** The Sovereignty Stack — three-layer integration of IFS parts work, somatic regulation, and business operating systems

**10. Aspiring Identity:** The Sovereign CEO — a woman who runs her business from self-trust instead of self-abandonment

---

## 2. HELL ISLAND vs. HEAVEN ISLAND

### Hell Island
You're making $300K but working 70-hour weeks, drinking wine to fall asleep because your nervous system won't shut off. Every Sunday your husband asks why you're working again, and you snap because you don't have an answer. You're charging half what your competitors charge because deep down, you're terrified that if you stop hustling, you'll discover you're not actually that talented.

### Heaven Island  
You run a business that serves you instead of consuming you. You charge premium prices without guilt because you know your worth isn't tied to your productivity. You take real time off—weekends, vacations, even a full month—because your business operates from systems, not your constant presence. You lead from a place of fullness, not depletion.

---

## 3. THE MAGNETIC MESSAGING STATEMENT

Women founders making $150K-$500K, are you struggling with working 70-hour weeks just to keep up, charging half what your competitors charge, and having zero time for anything but client work? And you feel resentful, exhausted, and lonely at the top? You need to stop trying to fix this with more discipline and better time management because the problem isn't your behavior—it's your nervous system running on self-abandonment. And start rewiring your identity with The Sovereignty Stack. When you do, you'll charge what you're worth and take real time off without guilt because you'll be operating from self-trust instead of proving your worth through overwork. You'll feel spacious, grounded, and self-trusting and become The Sovereign CEO who leads from fullness, not depletion.

---

## 4. PLATFORM VARIATIONS

### a) Ad Hook
Making $300K but working Sundays? Your pricing problem isn't a strategy problem—it's a nervous system problem.

### b) Social Media Post  
If you're a six-figure founder who can't stop working, the issue isn't discipline. It's that your nervous system is running on self-abandonment instead of self-trust. No amount of time blocking will fix what's happening in your body when you try to charge premium prices. The Sovereignty Stack rewires this at the identity level. Stop fixing symptoms. Start rewiring the source.

### c) Email Subject Line + Preview
**Subject:** Why you can't charge what you're worth  
**Preview:** (It's not what you think)

### d) Sales Page Hero
Six-figure founders: Stop trying to fix your pricing problem with better marketing. Your inability to charge what you're worth is a nervous system issue, not a strategy issue. The Sovereignty Stack rewires you to lead from self-trust instead of self-abandonment.

### e) Long-Form Opening
You're making $300K but working 70-hour weeks. Every Sunday your partner asks why you're working again, and you don't have an answer. You're charging half what your competitors charge, not because you don't know your value, but because your nervous system goes into fight-or-flight every time you think about raising prices. Here's the truth no one's telling you: your business problems aren't strategy problems—they're nervous system problems. You're running your entire operation from self-abandonment instead of self-trust. The Sovereignty Stack changes this at the identity level, so you can finally charge what you're worth and take real time off without your business collapsing.

---

## 5. ANTI-AVATAR STATEMENT

**This is NOT for you if:**
- You're looking for quick marketing tactics or another productivity system
- You think mindset work is "too woo" and you just need more discipline  
- You're not ready to look at the uncomfortable truth about why you undercharge
- You want someone to fix your business without you having to change
- You're making less than $150K and need basic business strategy first

---

## 6. "THIS IS FOR YOU IF..." STATEMENT

**This IS for you if:**
- You're making $150K-$500K but feel like your business owns you instead of serving you
- You know you're undercharging but your body goes into panic mode when you think about raising prices
- You're tired of working weekends and want to take real time off without everything falling apart
- You're ready to admit that your "discipline problem" is actually a nervous system problem
- You want to run your business from self-trust instead of constantly proving your worth through overwork
